core_content.markdown:
Our examples show that in matrix products the order of factors must always be observed very carefully.Otherwise 1matrix multiplication satisfies rules similar to those for r numbers, namely.



(a)

$$(k\mathbf{A})\mathbf{B}=k(\mathbf{A}\mathbf{B})=\mathbf{A}(k\mathbf{B})$$

(2)

written ABC

(d)

$$\mathbf{C}(\mathbf{A}+\mathbf{B})=\mathbf{C}\mathbf{A}+\mathbf{C}\mathbf{B}$$

provided  are s such that the e expressions on the left are c defined; here,is any scalar.(2b)is called the associative law. (2c)and (2d)are called the distributive laws.
